claim: splitToLimit returns the oversized chunk verbatim once depth > 10, so a single line that cannot be halved below MAX_TOKENS within 10 recursions is emitted over HARD_CHUNK_LIMIT.

chunk.tokenCount includes chunk.contextPrefix but splitChunk only divides chunk.content; the prefix is re-prepended to both halves and never split, so a chunk whose contextPrefix alone exceeds MAX_TOKENS is unsplittable and returned oversized.
Overlap lines are double-counted in both halves, so estimateTotalTokens grows after splitting; this can push a batch over softTokenLimit and skews budget math.
normalizePath does not lowercase, so on case-insensitive filesystems a finding path whose case differs from the chunk path never matches and a valid finding is silently dropped.
normalizePath does not resolve '..' segments or internal './', so relative paths like '../src/foo.ts' or 'src/./foo.ts' diverge from the chunk path and valid findings are dropped.
A finding with lineStart === undefined is returned early without a findingFingerprint, so downstream merger.ts cannot dedupe it and it leaks as a duplicate.
A non-integer lineStart (e.g. 12.5) is dropped entirely as if out of range instead of being clamped, discarding a valid position.
jaccardSimilarity returns 1 when both titles consist only of non-alphanumeric characters (both word sets empty), so two unrelated punctuation-only findings are merged as duplicates.
Array extraction takes the first '[' and last ']' of the LLM response, so stray or wrapping brackets produce invalid JSON and triage silently falls back to heuristicSelect.
